quick_skim.title: Low Complexity Neural Network Digital Predistortion of Wideband Power Amplifiers through Feature Selection
quick_skim.abstract: Due to the continuous increase in communication bandwidth and the use of highly efficient yet nonlinear power amplifiers, Digital Predistortion (DPD) algorithms are becoming increasingly complex. In particular, neural network (NN) based DPD approaches using Phase-Normalized NN architectures often incur substantially higher computational costs than widely deployed polynomial-based methods, such as the Memory Polynomial (MP) and Generalized Memory Polynomial (GMP) models. To bridge this gap between research performance and practical implementation, we propose a low-complexity Feature Selection NN DPD architecture. The proposed method employs an offline feature-engineering pipeline based on the Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO) and the Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (MRMR) algorithm to construct a compact and informative input representation. Using measured wideband FR3 power amplifier datasets that are publicly released with this work, we demonstrate up to 30% reduction in computational complexity while maintaining comparable linearization performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Low-complexity NN digital predistortion via feature selection, efficient NN for communication
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过特征选择的低复杂度神经网络数字预失真，高效通信NN设计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过特征选择的低复杂度神经网络数字预失真，高效通信NN设计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过特征选择的低复杂度神经网络数字预失真，高效通信NN设计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a low-complexity feature selection NN DPD architecture to match polynomial methods in cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对神经网络数字预失真（DPD）计算复杂度高于多项式方法的问题，提出基于离线特征工程的特征选择NN DPD架构。该方法使用LASSO回归构建特征工程流水线，仅保留重要特征，在宽带功放非线性校正中大幅降低计算量，同时保持优良线性化性能。该工作直接满足高效神经网络设计的用户需求。

quick_skim.title: HiFi-LLP: High-Fidelity, Low-Cost Latency Predictors with Confidence for Robust HW-NAS
quick_skim.abstract: With deep neural networks (DNNs) increasingly deployed on edge devices, hardware (HW)-aware optimization techniques--such as HW-aware compression and HW-aware neural architecture search (HW-NAS)--have become essential. These methods rely on real feedback from the target hardware to tailor DNN architectures for efficient deployment. While the search can be parallelized, latency measurements via hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) remain a bottleneck due to their sequential nature. Recent approaches use latency predictors to replace costly HIL feedback, but challenges persist: (1) platform-specific predictors often require tens of thousands of samples, and (2) inaccurate predictions can mislead the NAS process. To address this, we introduce HiFi-LLP, a high-fidelity, low-cost latency predictor based on graph attention networks, augmented with a confidence metric. HiFi-LLP outperforms prior platform-specific predictors by up to 9 percentage points (p.p.) in the 10% accuracy bound and achieves a Spearman's rank correlation of up to 0.996 across six devices in the LatBench dataset. We further propose a hybrid NAS framework that routes low-confidence predictions to HIL, achieving up to 8.6$\times$ speedup compared to typical NAS while maintaining a competitive Pareto front.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: method for efficient HW-NAS with low-cost latency predictors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于高效硬件感知神经架构搜索的低成本延迟预测器方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于高效硬件感知神经架构搜索的低成本延迟预测器方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于高效硬件感知神经架构搜索的低成本延迟预测器方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HiFi-LLP introduces high-fidelity, low-cost latency predictors with confidence estimates for robust HW-NAS, reducing the need for hardware-in-the-loop measurements.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 硬件感知神经架构搜索（HW-NAS）依赖大量硬件测量，成为效率瓶颈。该文提出HiFi-LLP，一种高保真、低成本延迟预测器，并引入置信度评估，避免不准确预测误导搜索过程。在多个边缘平台上的实验表明，仅需少量样本即可训练预测器，且搜索出的架构在延迟和准确率上均接近硬件实测方案。该方法为高效神经网络设计提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Evolutionary Algorithm-Guided LLMs for Physics-Informed Neural Network Design
quick_skim.abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) are unusually sensitive to interacting choices of architecture, activation, loss weighting, collocation, optimization, and constraint enforcement. Large language models (LLMs) can propose these choices, but independent recommendations do not accumulate experience from previously trained PINNs. We propose a closed-loop evolutionary algorithm that guides an LLM to generate complete, executable PINN configurations across generations, using measured training outcomes to determine subsequent search decisions. The algorithm maintains an evaluated population and lineage, applies parent-conditioned mutation and crossover, preserves elite and diverse solutions, rejects effective duplicates, and converts parent-relative successes and failures into the next-generation context supplied to the LLM. Every proposed configuration is executed directly under an exact optimizer-step budget. On a one-dimensional multiscale wave equation, two independent ten-generation runs trained 60 PINNs for 600,000 optimizer steps. In both runs, the best configuration appeared in the final generation, with best mean-squared error reduced by 2.97\% and 95.38\% relative to the initial population. The stronger run validated residual connections and increased depth on separate branches, combined them in a later generation, and then refined width and collocation density. It also revealed that low solution error can coexist with a high PDE residual. These results demonstrate the feasibility of evolutionary-algorithm-guided LLMs for PINN design on a controlled PDE while motivating broader, physics-aware evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: method for designing PINNs using EA and LLM, transferable to communication systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用进化算法和LLM设计PINN的方法，可迁移至通信系统
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用进化算法和LLM设计PINN的方法，可迁移至通信系统
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用进化算法和LLM设计PINN的方法，可迁移至通信系统
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EA-guided LLM generates complete PINN configurations optimized over generations, applicable to any domain including communication.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对物理信息神经网络(PINN)对架构、激活函数等超参数高度敏感的问题，提出闭环进化算法引导LLM生成完整可执行PINN配置。算法在代际间利用训练结果指导搜索，保留精英和多样化解，能自动发现高性能PINN设计。该方法可直接迁移到通信信道建模等物理应用场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对物理信息神经网络(PINN)对架构、激活函数等超参数高度敏感的问题，提出闭环进化算法引导LLM生成完整可执行PINN配置。算法在代际间利用训练结果指导搜索，保留精英和多样化解，能自动发现高性能PINN设计。该方法可直接迁移到通信信道建模等物理应用场景。

quick_skim.title: Trainable Spline Representations for Physics-Informed Learning
quick_skim.abstract: This work introduces Physics-Informed Splines (PI-Splines), a structured spline-based architecture for physics-informed learning. Instead of representing the solution of a differential equation with a neural network, PI-Splines directly parametrize the unknown field through a tensor-product B-spline expansion with trainable control coefficients. This formulation preserves the residual-based training paradigm of Physics-Informed Neural Networks while providing compact support, explicit smoothness control, analytical derivatives, and a direct geometric interpretation of the trainable parameters. When compatible with the spline representation, boundary conditions can be imposed strongly by fixing suitable boundary control coefficients. The proposed method is evaluated on several benchmark problems of increasing difficulty and compared with standard physics-informed frameworks under matched governing equations, collocation sets, loss terms, and optimization procedures, so as to isolate the effect of the approximation architecture. Numerical experiments show that PI-Splines provide a competitive and stable alternative to neural physics-informed architectures, particularly in settings where structured representations, locality, and parameter efficiency are desirable.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Physics-informed splines for learning PDEs, applicable to communication channel modeling as PINN variant
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 物理信息样条用于PDE学习，可作为通信信道建模中PINN的变体
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 物理信息样条用于PDE学习，可作为通信信道建模中PINN的变体
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 物理信息样条用于PDE学习，可作为通信信道建模中PINN的变体
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PI-Splines, a spline-based architecture for physics-informed learning, preserving PINN training paradigm.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对物理信息神经网络（PINN）在求解微分方程时参数解释性弱、边界条件处理复杂的问题，提出基于张量积B样条展开的物理信息样条（PI-Splines）。该方法将未知场直接参数化为可训练控制系数的样条，保留残差训练范式，同时提供显式光滑度控制和解析导数。实验证明其在多个PDE问题上具有竞争力，为物理信息学习提供了新架构，可潜在用于通信信道建模。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对物理信息神经网络（PINN）在求解微分方程时参数解释性弱、边界条件处理复杂的问题，提出基于张量积B样条展开的物理信息样条（PI-Splines）。该方法将未知场直接参数化为可训练控制系数的样条，保留残差训练范式，同时提供显式光滑度控制和解析导数。实验证明其在多个PDE问题上具有竞争力，为物理信息学习提供了新架构，可潜在用于通信信道建模。
